authoritative_sources.title: FY 2026 HMIS Data Standards Manual
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD publishes HMIS data standards for HMIS leads, end users, and federal partner programs, making the manual authoritative for homelessness project, client, enrollment, exit, service, and destination record definitions.

authoritative_sources.title: HDX Guides: HIC and PIT Count
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD Exchange HDX guidance governs Housing Inventory Count and Point-in-Time Count reporting workflow, making it authoritative for CoC inventory and unsheltered or sheltered count analytics.

authoritative_sources.title: CoC System Performance Measures
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD defines the CoC system performance measure workflow and metric logic, making this authoritative for exits to permanent housing, returns to homelessness, length-of-time homeless, and related homelessness-system analytics.
used_for: blocked-state handling when HMIS extracts are stale

authoritative_sources.title: Integrated Disbursement and Information System (IDIS)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD identifies IDIS as the official CPD formula grant drawdown and reporting system, making it authoritative for CDBG, HOME, ESG, HOPWA activity, accomplishment, beneficiary, and financial-reporting evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: eCon Planning Suite
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's eCon Planning Suite supports Consolidated Plan, Annual Action Plan, and CAPER workflow, making it authoritative for planning, performance, and public-reporting artifact expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: Comprehensive Housing Affordability Strategy (CHAS) Data
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD CHAS is the public HUD baseline for local housing needs, affordability, cost burden, tenure, household type, and income-band analysis used in consolidated planning.
used_for: cost burden and severe cost burden terms

authoritative_sources.title: Low-Income Housing Tax Credit Database
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD USER publishes the national LIHTC property database and documentation, making it authoritative public evidence for LIHTC property, unit, placed-in-service, and geography analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: affordable housing inventory and preservation analysis

authoritative_sources.title: Home Mortgage Disclosure Act (HMDA) Data
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FFIEC and CFPB administer public HMDA data and reporting guidance, making the source authoritative for mortgage application, action-taken, tract, and disclosure-modified loan-level housing access analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: mortgage access record collection and disclosure review

authoritative_sources.title: American Community Survey Information Guide and Handbooks
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Census Bureau operates ACS and publishes official guidance for using estimates, geographies, and margins of error, making it authoritative for housing needs, demographic, and cost-burden inputs that rely on ACS.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: public demographic and housing estimate handling

authoritative_sources.title: FHFA House Price Index
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FHFA publishes the official federal House Price Index, making it authoritative for public house-price benchmark use and for caveating index geography, transaction coverage, and methodology limits.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: market benchmark selection and caveat design

authoritative_sources.title: Community Planning and Development Monitoring Handbook 6509.2
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's CPD monitoring handbook defines monitoring, file review, findings, concerns, and corrective-action expectations for community development programs, making it authoritative for evidence and control posture.

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC KPI guidance is authoritative for defining, contextualizing, caveating, and updating operational metrics when they move toward executive, investor-style, or external disclosure.

workflow_stages.name: request framing and housing context selection
workflow_stages.description: Confirm whether the request concerns homelessness services, community development grants, affordable housing compliance, development pipeline, property operations, mortgage access, or market benchmarking; capture audience, cadence, jurisdiction, geography, program, and publication posture.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect HMIS, HDX, PIT, HIC, SPM, IDIS, DRGR, CAPER, CHAS, ACS, LIHTC, HMDA, FHFA HPI, property, pipeline, finance, GIS, and tenant data dictionaries with system, date, geography, owner, and refresh lineage.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: metric definition and denominator selection
workflow_stages.description: Define housing metric numerators, denominators, exclusions, AMI or income-limit vintage, geography, program year, household or unit uniqueness, project status, source hierarchy, suppression rules, and action thresholds.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: record reconciliation and confidence rating
workflow_stages.description: Compare source values across HMIS, HDX, IDIS, DRGR, property management, development pipeline, LIHTC, HMDA, ACS, CHAS, finance, and local systems; preserve conflicts and assign confidence instead of forcing one number.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: analysis or report package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Assemble descriptive housing analysis with source logic, metric definitions, caveats, uncertainty, geography and vintage notes, data-quality exceptions, and next-evidence requests.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: narrative synthesis and disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Stop descriptive analysis from becoming unsupported causal, forecast, fair-housing, policy, compliance, eligibility, funding, investor-style, or public-facing claims without explicit evidence and approvals.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: exception escalation and adjacent-owner routing
workflow_stages.description: Route eligibility, fair housing, legal, compliance, funding, underwriting, appraisal, project approval, privacy, low-cell disclosure, and formal submission requests to the correct owner with evidence preserved.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: HMIS client, project, enrollment, exit, income, service, destination, and APR records
HDX, PIT, HIC, ESG CAPER, and CoC system performance measure extracts
IDIS activity, accomplishment, beneficiary, draw, national objective, and program-year reports
DRGR grant, activity, performance, draw, and CDBG-DR reporting extracts
eCon Planning Suite, Consolidated Plan, Annual Action Plan, CAPER, and public comment artifacts
CHAS, ACS, decennial census, geography crosswalk, GIS, parcel, zoning, permit, and certificate of occupancy records
LIHTC public database, Form 8609 reference, rent and income limit vintage, unit mix, and compliance-period context
HMDA loan application register, action taken, tract, applicant, loan purpose, occupancy, lien, and disclosure-modified data
property management, rent roll, occupancy, subsidy, voucher, waitlist, referral, inspection, maintenance, and ledger records
development pipeline, acquisition, entitlement, construction, funding commitment, grant, loan, tax credit, project budget, pro forma, schedule, and closing records
metric dictionary, source hierarchy, data-quality exception register, reconciliation note, caveat register, disclosure-risk note, and adjacent-owner handoff package
systems_of_record: property management and rent roll system
subsidy, voucher, waitlist, and referral systems
development pipeline and project management system
finance, loan, grant, tax credit, and pro forma systems
LIHTC compliance and asset management system
HMDA public data platform or lender compliance system
Census, CHAS, ACS, FHFA HPI, GIS, parcel, zoning, permit, and market data repositories
governed BI, data warehouse, metadata catalog, data-quality, privacy, approval, and document-control systems

allowed: Classify requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing housing prerequisites.
Select source hierarchy, metric definitions, geography, vintage, denominator, exclusion, refresh, and caveat logic for housing analysis.
Prepare evidence-backed descriptive analysis, reporting packages, reconciliation notes, exception notes, and next-evidence requests.
Flag when data quality, metric ambiguity, low-cell risk, privacy risk, disclosure risk, source conflict, or unsupported benchmark posture prevents confident reporting.
escalate: Missing jurisdiction, program, property, project, geography, source hierarchy, retrieval hook, data-sharing agreement, suppression rule, or publication approval.
Conflicting HUD program guidance, tenant policy, local jurisdiction instruction, funder rule, or public benchmark definition materially changes the answer.

Request implicates HMIS privacy, fair housing, tenant rights, eligibility, grant compliance, LIHTC compliance, mortgage lending compliance, underwriting, appraisal, financing, construction, safety, or formal disclosure authority.
refuse: Determine eligibility, legal rights, fair-housing compliance, underwriting, appraisal, funding approval, program compliance, tax credit compliance, or project feasibility.
Certify HUD, CoC, CDBG, HOME, ESG, DRGR, HMDA, LIHTC, investor, lender, board, regulator, or public-facing submissions.
Publish unsupported causal, benchmark, forecast, displacement, neighborhood-stabilization, discrimination, policy, compliance, or disclosure claims.

domain_failure_modes: Generic analytics output ignores housing artifacts such as HMIS, HDX, IDIS, CAPER, CHAS, ACS, LIHTC, HMDA, property, pipeline, subsidy, waitlist, geography, program year, and affordability restriction records.
Metric definition changes silently between committed, completed, placed-in-service, restricted, occupied, leased, and household-served units.
AMI band, income-limit vintage, rent-limit vintage, geography, household uniqueness, or ACS margin-of-error handling materially changes the result.
HMIS, HIC, PIT, SPM, IDIS, DRGR, CAPER, property, finance, LIHTC, HMDA, and public benchmark records conflict and the lane asserts one value without source-precedence approval.
Public source data are stale, privacy-modified, low-cell, or not comparable, but the output implies precise or causal conclusions.

Housing analytics drifts into eligibility, fair housing, legal, grant compliance, tax credit compliance, lending compliance, underwriting, appraisal, funding, development feasibility, or certification decisions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: tenant-specific source precedence and publication approval
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline identifies common authoritative sources, but tenants must provide local source hierarchy, data-sharing agreements, suppression policy, jurisdiction, and signoff owners before publication.
Local eligibility rules, fair-housing interpretations, funder agreements, QAP terms, and program manuals can materially change outcomes and must be retrieved or escalated rather than guessed.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because unresolved local rules are handled as runtime prerequisites and refusal or escalation conditions.
The source set covers workflow, evidence, terminology, metric definition, benchmark caveats, privacy or disclosure risk, and failure handling for housing analytics.
